title: Fluffy Spicy Greek Eggplant Stir-fry
description: This sour and fluffy eggplant dish features Shaoxing wine and a tadka for a unique and crowd-pleasing flavor that's perfect as an appetizer or side dish.
instructions: Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C). Cut the eggplant into cubes, drizzle with olive oil and a pinch of salt. Roast for about 20 minutes until golden brown.
While the eggplant is roasting, prepare the sauce by crushing garlic cloves and mixing them with soy sauce, Shaoxing wine, lemon juice, and a few pinches of salt in a small bowl.
Once the eggplant is cooked, let it cool slightly. Slice it into thin rounds and place on a plate.
Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a pan over medium heat. Add sliced garlic and cook for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour the sauce mixture into the pan with the garlic. Stir well to combine and let it simmer for about 1-2 minutes until slightly thickened.
Add roasted eggplant slices to the pan, tossing them in the sauce to coat evenly. Let cook for an additional minute allowing the flavors to meld together.
Garnish with coriander leaves if using. Serve hot or warm as a side dish with steamed rice or bread.
